candidate_summary: K orthogonal gradient subspaces {S_1..S_K} arranged in ring; project gradient onto S_{(t-1) mod K +1} per step (CCW); rhythm-anchor alignment direction e_anchor; heart-toward-fire constraint cos(θ, e_anchor)>τ; spiral-inward annealed subspace radius. Inspired by Cherokee stomp dance counter-clockwise ring.
content_words: K-subspace counter-clockwise ring traversal gradient projection

results.name: SubTrack++ Grassmannian Subspace Tracking
results.rationale: Grassmannian subspace tracking with orthonormality preservation overlaps with K orthogonal subspaces.

results.name: Continual Gradient Low-Rank Projection ACL 2025
results.rationale: Gradient projection onto low-rank subspaces matches projection-per-step.

results.name: Lotus Adaptive Subspace Switching
results.rationale: Adaptive switching between subspaces closely parallels S_{t mod K} cyclic projection; sem+func >0.7.

results.name: OGPSA Orthogonal Gradient Safety Alignment
results.rationale: Orthogonal gradient with alignment constraint mirrors e_anchor cos(θ)>τ heart-toward-fire constraint.

results.name: Randomized Gradient Subspaces GrassWalk/GrassJump
results.rationale: Subspace traversal via Grassmann walks/jumps echoes ring-traversal of K subspaces.

results.name: GuardSpace Safety-Sensitive Subspace Null
results.rationale: Null-space form + safety-sensitive subspace projection directly matches null-space-traversal form with alignment constraint.

results.name: Fira Full-Rank Training Low-Rank Constraint
results.rationale: Low-rank constraint on gradients overlaps weakly with subspace projection.

results.name: Low-Rank Gradient Subspace Stabilization ICML 2025
results.rationale: Subspace stabilization with annealed radius parallels spiral-inward annealed subspace radius.

decision_rationale: All 8 prior art entries register as hits. Lotus, OGPSA, and GuardSpace cross 0.7 threshold on sem/func. Candidate's distinguishing CCW-ring ordering + rhythm-anchor cos-constraint + spiral-inward annealed radius are each individually anticipated by prior art. Verdict: FAIL.
